compiled_state.title: As a developer, review findings can identify structurally fragile code as a distinct severity that blocks merge
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a new fragile finding severity to the review pipeline finding model that blocks merge, alongside critical, important, and minor.
**Why**: Currently fragile workarounds are classified as minor findings and pass through review, allowing structurally weak code into the codebase.
**Scope**:
- IN: fragile severity enum in all validators, merge-blocking behavior, score-severity consistency rules, resolution workflow triage, reviewer prompt schema examples
- OUT: Reviewer prompt instructions for when to use fragile (Story 2). ESCALATE_REVIEW signal (Story 3).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a reviewer-findings.json file containing a finding with severity fragile passes schema validation in validate-review-output.sh, write-reviewer-findings.sh, and record-review.sh without errors
  ← Satisfies: "A new finding severity fragile is added that blocks merge like important"
- When this story is complete, a fragile finding causes the review pass/fail gate in record-review.sh to emit STATUS=failed, blocking the commit
  ← Satisfies: "A new finding severity fragile is added that blocks merge like important"
- When this story is complete, the score-severity consistency validator accepts fragile findings with score 3 or below (same floor as important) and rejects fragile findings with score 4 or above
  ← Satisfies: "A new finding severity fragile is added that blocks merge like important"
- When this story is complete, the resolution workflow triage routes fragile findings to Fix or Defend only — fragile findings cannot be Deferred
  ← Satisfies: "A new finding severity fragile is added that blocks merge like important"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Atomic schema update — fragile must be added to all severity enums simultaneously: validate-review-output.sh (3 locations: code-review-dispatch at line 259, review-protocol at line 357, plan-review at line 884), record-review.sh (line 192), and write-reviewer-findings.sh
- [Maintainability] Resolution workflow (review-fix-dispatch.md) triage table must explicitly route fragile — cannot be deferrable like minor
- [Adversarial] write-reviewer-findings.sh is an independent gatekeeper not listed in the original file scope — must be updated or fragile findings cannot be written to disk at all
- [Adversarial] Score-severity consistency must define an exact mapping for fragile, not a range — the existing validator uses exact severity-to-score mappings (minor→4, important→3, critical→1-2); fragile maps to score 3 (same as important)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
